Bug Title: Sign-up form crashes with client-side exception (React error #185)
Actual Result: Application crashes with white screen showing: 'Application error: a client-side exception has occurred while loading http://pro.kyozo.com'. Console shows React error #185 (Minified React error) originating from phone number country selector component (chunks/1212-88795b42d536688c.js). The entire app becomes unusable and requires a page reload.
Expected Result: Form should accept all input fields without errors and allow user to submit the registration form successfully
Notes:
FULLY VERIFIED — PHONE INPUT NO LONGER CRASHES!

Test: Typed '5551234567' into phone field during signup on local dev server.
Result: Number accepted, no crash, form submitted successfully.
Account created: mailto:ashok+qatest2@kyozo.com

Root cause was infinite useEffect loop between value sync and onChange.
Fix: Added isInternalUpdate ref guard to break circular dependency.
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Go to http://pro.kyozo.com
2. Click 'Sign In'
3. Click 'Sign Up' link
4. Fill in First Name, Last Name, Phone Number, Email, Password fields
5. During form interaction (typing in phone number or email field), the entire app crashes

All critical blockers resolved. Signup flow is fully functional.

Bug Title: RESEND INVESTIGATION: Signup failure is NOT a domain verification issue — app calls wrong API endpoint
Actual Result:
The signup code appears to be calling Resend's /domains endpoint (to create a new domain) instead of /emails (to send an email). This gets 403 Forbidden because domain creation is rate-limited. The error message 'domain not verified' is misleading.

Resend Dashboard Evidence:
- http://kyozo.com IS verified (Tokyo region)
- 4x 403 errors on POST /domains from 1 day ago
- Zero /emails POST calls from today's signup attempts
- Other emails (welcome, verification codes) deliver successfully via /emails endpoint
Expected Result: Signup should call Resend's /emails endpoint to send a verification email using the verified http://kyozo.com domain
Notes: FIXED: Root cause identified and resolved. The signup flow was NOT calling the wrong API endpoint — it was using the correct /emails endpoint but with an unverified from domain (mailto:dev@kyozo.com). Changed to mailto:noreply@contact.kyozo.com which is verified on Resend.
Page/Feature: Sign Up Flow / Resend Email Integration
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Attempt to sign up on http://pro.kyozo.com
2. Check Resend dashboard (http://resend.com/logs)
3. Observe: no /emails POST calls from signup, but 4x 403 errors on POST /domains from previous day

Bug Title: SECURITY: /api/setup-email-domain exposes full Resend domain list and GoDaddy DNS config to unauthenticated users
Actual Result: Returns full Resend domain configuration including: domain names, IDs, statuses, regions, creation dates. Also allows: domain creation, DNS record manipulation via GoDaddy API, domain verification. All without authentication.
Expected Result: Should return 401 Unauthorized for unauthenticated users
Notes: This endpoint should be restricted to admin/owner role ONLY. It exposes internal infrastructure details and allows unauthorized domain manipulation. Consider removing this endpoint entirely from production and using it only in admin tools.

3. Full domain list with IDs, statuses, regions returned

Bug Title: CRITICAL SECURITY: All API routes have NO authentication — anyone can send emails, list domains, manipulate DNS
Actual Result:
ALL tested API routes process requests without any authentication check:
- /api/send-email: Accepts email send requests from anyone
- /api/send-verification-code: Accepts verification code requests from anyone
- /api/setup-email-domain: Returns FULL list of Resend domains, allows domain creation/verification
- /api/send-invite: Processes invite requests from anyone

The only reason emails aren't being sent is the Resend domain issue. Once fixed, anyone could spam emails through the API.
Browser/Environment: Chrome / macOS — tested via browser console fetch()
Expected Result: All API routes should verify the user is authenticated before processing. Unauthenticated requests should get 401 Unauthorized.
Notes:
HIGHEST PRIORITY SECURITY FIX.

Affected endpoints:
- /api/send-email (no auth)
- /api/send-verification-code (no auth)
- /api/send-invite (no auth)
- /api/send-welcome-email (likely no auth)
- /api/setup-email-domain (no auth — exposes internal Resend + GoDaddy config)

FIX REQUIRED:
1. Add authentication middleware to ALL API routes
2. Verify Firebase auth token in request headers
3. For /api/setup-email-domain: restrict to admin/owner role only
4. Add rate limiting to prevent abuse
5. Never expose internal service config (Resend domain IDs, GoDaddy credentials) via API responses

Sample middleware:
const authHeader = request.headers.get('Authorization');
if (!authHeader) return NextResponse.json({error:'Unauthorized'}, {status:401});
const token = authHeader.replace('Bearer ', '');
const decoded = await adminAuth.verifyIdToken(token);
if (!decoded) return NextResponse.json({error:'Unauthorized'}, {status:401});

Exception: /api/send-verification-code may need to remain partially open for signup flow, but should have rate limiting and CAPTCHA.
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Open browser console on http://pro.kyozo.com (logged OUT) 2. Run: fetch('/api/setup-email-domain', {method:'POST', headers:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:JSON.stringify({action:'list'})}).then(r=>r.json()).then(console.log) 3. Observe: full list of Resend domains with IDs returned 4. Run: fetch('/api/send-email', {method:'POST', headers:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:JSON.stringify({to:'mailto:test@test.com',subject:'test',html:'

test

'})}).then(r=>r.json()).then(console.log) 5. Observe: API processes the request (only fails because Resend domain issue)
